personas.label: Skeptical operator
personas.motivation: save time and avoid fragile tools
personas.coreObjection: This sounds like setup complexity disguised as automation.
personas.contentLens: Skeptical operator evaluating fun, curated networking events in New York where interesting people meet through a social hospitality experience; sell event tickets and grow the community around the Out Of Office brand

personas.label: Cost-sensitive founder
personas.coreObjection: Local-first is attractive, but quality and maintenance worry me.
personas.conversionTrigger: specific hardware expectations and realistic limits
personas.contentLens: Cost-sensitive founder evaluating fun, curated networking events in New York where interesting people meet through a social hospitality experience; sell event tickets and grow the community around the Out Of Office brand

personas.label: Creative strategist
personas.coreObjection: The content may become generic if the system does not learn taste.
personas.conversionTrigger: memory from feedback and strong examples
personas.contentLens: Creative strategist evaluating fun, curated networking events in New York where interesting people meet through a social hospitality experience; sell event tickets and grow the community around the Out Of Office brand

personas.label: Agency delivery lead
personas.coreObjection: Approval, revisions, and client-specific voice need to be robust.
personas.conversionTrigger: clear review states and client-ready artifacts
personas.contentLens: Agency delivery lead evaluating fun, curated networking events in New York where interesting people meet through a social hospitality experience; sell event tickets and grow the community around the Out Of Office brand

personas.label: Technical early adopter
personas.motivation: own the stack and customize agents
personas.coreObjection: I need proof that integrations are modular and not locked to a SaaS.
personas.conversionTrigger: repo structure, local commands, and extension points
personas.contentLens: Technical early adopter evaluating fun, curated networking events in New York where interesting people meet through a social hospitality experience; sell event tickets and grow the community around the Out Of Office brand

drafts.title: Post 1
drafts.body:
Hook: Stop paying for a social stack before your content system is even working.

Body: corporate professionals, founders, operators, creatives, consultants, finance and tech workers, and socially ambitious newcomers in New York who want high-quality networking without a stiff conference vibe need a repeatable local-first workflow: strategy, drafts, review, and publishing discipline before more SaaS.

CTA: Save this and review your current content workflow.

evaluations.likelyReaction: Interesting, but show me how this works in a real corporate professionals workflow.
evaluations.mainObjection: Needs concrete proof or before/after example.
evaluations.recommendedFix: Add a concrete proof point for the Skeptical operator: screenshot, before/after, workflow artifact, or mini case.
